title: Production Shift Handover Report Generation
spl:
index=mfg sourcetype="mes:production"
| eval shift=case(hour(_time)>=6 AND hour(_time)<14,"A", hour(_time)>=14 AND hour(_time)<22,"B", true(),"C")
| bin _time span=8h aligntime=@d+6h
| stats sum(units_good) as units, sum(downtime_min) as down_m, sum(scrap_units) as scrap by line_id, shift, _time
| eval scrap_rate=if(units+scrap>0, 100*scrap/(units+scrap), null)
| sort _time, line_id, shift
| table _time, line_id, shift, units, down_m, scrap, scrap_rate
description: Consistent shift reports reduce tacit knowledge loss and accelerate startup; KPI rollups improve accountability across crews on 24/7 lines.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Production Shift Handover Report Generation to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Schedule a saved search at shift change to email PDF or post to Teams via alert action. Pull `shift_id` from MES when available instead of inferred buckets. Optional append subsearch for top downtime reasons from OPC alarms. Store outputs in summary index for trend comparison. **Domain context:** Shift handover is a known safety and quality interface in 24/7 operations (ISA-18 / human factors); align shift boundaries to plant calendar and collective agreements. **Splunk:** `hour(_time)` uses indexer TZ unless search TZ is set—use explicit `shift_id` from MES when possible; `aligntime` on `bin` may not match all plants’ shift start times.
visualization: Table (shift KPI summary), Column chart (units by shift), Line chart (scrap rate trend), Single value (plant output per shift).
knownFalsePositives: Handover metric gaps on holiday calendars, data-entry lag from the floor, or template changes the operations manager rolled out for the new KPI review.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Intelligence, MES HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=mfg` `sourcetype="mes:production"` (line_id, units_good, downtime_min, scrap_units, shift_id).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Schedule a saved search at shift change to email PDF or post to Teams via alert action. Pull `shift_id` from MES when available instead of inferred buckets. Optional append subsearch for top downtime reasons from OPC alarms. Store outputs in summary index for trend comparison. **Domain context:** Shift handover is a known safety and quality interface in 24/7 operations (ISA-18 / human factors); align shift boundaries to plant calendar and collective agreements. **Splunk:** `hour(_time)` uses ind…

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=mfg sourcetype="mes:production"
| eval shift=case(hour(_time)>=6 AND hour(_time)<14,"A", hour(_time)>=14 AND hour(_time)<22,"B", true(),"C")
| bin _time span=8h aligntime=@d+6h
| stats sum(units_good) as units, sum(downtime_min) as down_m, sum(scrap_units) as scrap by line_id, shift, _time
| eval scrap_rate=if(units+scrap>0, 100*scrap/(units+scrap), null)
| sort _time, line_id, shift
| table _time, line_id, shift, units, down_m, scrap, scrap_rate
```

Understanding this SPL

**Production Shift Handover Report Generation** — Consistent shift reports reduce tacit knowledge loss and accelerate startup; KPI rollups improve accountability across crews on 24/7 lines.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=mfg` `sourcetype="mes:production"` (line_id, units_good, downtime_min, scrap_units, shift_id). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Intelligence, MES HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: mfg; **sourcetype**: mes:production. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=mfg, sourcetype="mes:production". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **shift** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by line_id, shift, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **scrap_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Production Shift Handover Report Generation**): table _time, line_id, shift, units, down_m, scrap, scrap_rate


Step 3 — Validate
Compare calculated buckets with the MES shift report or historian (for example PI or Proficy) for the same line and period. Confirm planned versus run time matches the routing master used by production.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (shift KPI summary), Column chart (units by shift), Line chart (scrap rate trend), Single value (plant output per shift).
grandmaExplanation: We watch what each shift hands to the next. We help you catch holes before safety or quality handoffs fail.
